items.title: Match of the Day
items.summary: Gabby Logan presents highlights of Sunday’s four Premier League games.

items.title: Samenvatting: Ajax en NEC bezorgen elkaar averij in strijd om plek twee
items.summary: Ajax en NEC hebben averij opgelopen in de strijd om plek twee in de Eredivisie. De twee concurrenten speelden gelijk tegen elkaar, na doelpunten van Mika Godts en Sami Ouaissa in de Johan Cruijff Arena.

items.title: Samenvatting: PSV spoelt Eredivisie-kater weg tegen SC Heerenveen
items.summary: PSV heeft de kater die het overhield aan de nederlaag tegen FC Volendam weggespoeld. Ricardo Pepi maakte zijn rentree in de met 3-1 gewonnen wedstrijd van SC Heerenveen.

items.title: Samenvatting: Feyenoord wint debuutwedstrijd Sterling nipt van Telstar
items.summary: Feyenoord boekte zondagavond zijn derde overwinning op rij in de Eredivisie. Anis Hadj Moussa bezorgde de Rotterdammers een minimale zege op Telstar in een wedstrijd waarin Raheem Sterling debuteerde.

items.title: 'NEC is alles wat Ajax wil zijn'
items.summary: Ajax heeft geen goede indruk achtergelaten bij de analytici van Studio Voetbal . De Amsterdammers hadden het zaterdag lastig met het attractieve voetbal van NEC.

items.title: Van Persie geeft hoopvolle update over hoofdblessure St. Juste
items.summary: Robin van Persie heeft een update gegeven over Jeremiah St. Juste. De verdediger van Feyenoord ging tegen Telstar noodgedwongen naar de kant vanwege een hoofdblessure, maar maakt het nu goed. 'We moeten de komende dagen even afwachten hoe hij er uit is gekomen.'

items.title: Michael Carrick says he has not spoken to Jim Ratcliffe since last month
items.summary: Manchester United manager last saw Ratcliffe on 25 January ‘I’m fine with that,’ says Carrick, in buildup to Everton game Michael Carrick has revealed he and his Manchester United squad have not received an apology or a message of any sort from Sir Jim Ratcliffe after his claim that the United Kingdom has been “colonised by immigrants”. Ratcliffe, United’s largest single shareholder and head of the club’s football policy, made the comments during a Sky News interview on 11 February. The outcry was strong and immediate, leading the 73-year-old to say the next day he was sorry if his “choice of language has offended some people in the UK and Europe”. Continue reading...

items.title: Arsenal answer title pressure with resounding derby win at Tottenham
items.summary: Despite the growing pressure in the title race, Mikel Arteta's team responded with character to claim a victory over their local rivals and deliver a message to their critics.

items.title: VAR review: Analyzing VAR drama for Liverpool's Mac Allister
items.summary: Alexis Mac Allister was involved in VAR decisions twice vs. Nottingham Forest. But what happened?

items.title: Littler defies nine-dart Van Veen to win first-ever Poland Darts Open
items.summary: World No 1 Luke Littler defied a perfect leg from Dutchman Gian van Veen to win the first-ever Poland Darts Open on Sunday.

items.title: Wolves disgusted by 'abhorrent' Arokodare abuse
items.summary: Wolves have reported "abhorrent and unlawful" racist abuse against striker Tolu Arokodare, who has become the latest Premier League player to be subjected to discriminatory social media attacks.

items.title: Arteta: 'Ashamed' Arsenal showed grit vs. Spurs
items.summary: Mikel Arteta said Arsenal "showed what we're made of" in Sunday's 4-1 win at Tottenham after feeling "ashamed" by their previous draw at Wolves.

items.title: MLS: Messi did not break policy in alleged video
items.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating allegations that Lionel Messi gained unauthorized access to the officials' locker room following the match between Inter Miami and LAFC on Saturday at the LA Memorial Coliseum.

items.title: Beating Tottenham can be a ‘turning point’ for Arsenal, says proud Arteta
items.summary: Manager delighted with reaction to midweek draw Arsenal go five points clear of Manchester City at top Mikel Arteta said that he could not be “prouder or happier” after his Arsenal side restored their five-point lead at the top of the Premier League table with a 4-1 win against Tottenham on Sunday that he suggested could be a “turning point” in their season. This was an emphatic response after squandering a 2-0 lead at Wolves on Wednesday, a result that means City will win the league if they win all their remaining 11 games this season. Continue reading...

items.title: Nev: Arsenal answered questions in Spurs win and withstood title race pressure
items.summary: Gary Neville says Arsenal have answered questions in their win at Tottenham, and withstood the pressure put on them by Manchester City in the Premier League title race.

items.title: Gyokeres has best game for Arsenal, so why was it so good?
items.summary: There are still a lot of questions about Arsenal, the title race and what is to come from them in the run-in this season. But one thing is absolutely certain: Viktor Gyokeres had his best game for the club against Tottenham.

items.title: Eze torments Spurs again as leaders Arsenal win north London derby
items.summary: Eberechi Eze torments Tottenham Hotspur again as Arsenal made a title statement of intent by restoring their lead at the top of the Premier League table to five points with victory in the north London derby.

items.title: Mac Allister scores added-time winner as Liverpool beat Forest
items.summary: Alexis Mac Allister scores a 97th-minute winner as Liverpool beat Nottingham Forest in a chaotic finish at the City Ground.

items.title: Jimenez scores twice as Fulham leapfrog Sunderland into top half of table
items.summary: Raul Jimenez scores twice as Fulham end a run of three consecutive defeats, with a 3-1 win over Sunderland at the Stadium of Light in the Premier League.

items.title: Guessand gives Palace much-needed win against 10-man Wolves
items.summary: Evann Guessand scores a late winner as Crystal Palace beat 10-man Wolves to claim their first home win since early November.
